title: Human Development Index groups
subtitle: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.
caption:
Human Development Index groups

The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of countries categorized into four Human Development Index groups for the year 2023. The legend defines these categories using a color scale ranging from red for "Low," yellow for "Medium," light blue for "High," and dark blue for "Very high," with gray hatching indicating areas where no data is available. The "Very high" category, represented by dark blue, encompasses the majority of North America, including Canada and the United States, as well as most of Western and Northern Europe, Russia, Australia, and New Zealand. The "High" category, shown in light blue, covers a significant portion of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, along with Mexico, China, and several nations in Eastern Europe and the Middle East. The "Medium" group, colored yellow, includes India, Indonesia, and a band of countries across North Africa and parts of Southeast Asia. The "Low" category, depicted in red, is concentrated primarily in Sub-Saharan Africa, forming a large contiguous block across the central and western regions of the continent, with Yemen also falling into this group. Greenland is the only major landmass marked with the "No data" pattern. The visual distribution shows a distinct geographic clustering, with the highest development levels concentrated in the Northern Hemisphere's temperate zones and Oceania, while the lowest levels are predominantly located in the African continent.
